channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: DOGE defeat: Congress has rejected most of Trump's spending cuts
item.description: President Trump and his DOGE swung for the fences last year in calling for big spending cuts, but with Congress now done with most of the 2026 budget ...

item.title: Ed O’Keefe’s Trump Derangement Is So Bad, Even The White House Press Corps Is Laughing At Him
item.description: CBS News’ Ed O’Keefe learned a new concept Wednesday: facts. During Wednesday’s White House press briefing, O’Keefe asked White House Press Secretary ...

item.title: In Politics, Social Media Has Supplanted Experience
item.description: The clip is the product. Governance is incidental.

item.title: AI is running out of power. Space won’t be an escape hatch for decades
item.description: The physics mostly work. The timelines, economics, and infrastructure don’t—at least not yet.

item.title: Sue-Pervised Retirement
item.description: Rampant litigation against 401(k) sponsors is narrowing employees’ investment choices and eroding their retirement wealth.

item.title: 'Karen' dodges charges; Trump eyes library agency; W.Va. vaccine fight
item.description: A Maryland school board activist avoids charges after going viral, the Trump administration eyes a little-known library agency for cultural messaging,...

item.title: Trump's refusal to invite all governors to a White House meeting prompts group to back out
item.description: The National Governors Association is pulling out of an annual meeting at the White House after President Donald Trump declined to invite two Democrat...

item.title: EU Parliament Says Men Are Women And Anyone Can Get Pregnant
item.description: The EU Parliament has done great harm. This is far more than progressive virtue signaling. We hear warnings of transhumanism.

item.title: Bringing Back The Talking Filibuster Could Do More To Save America Than Just Passing The SAVE Act
item.description: The talking filibuster is worth trying. It is worth a public accounting of whether this Senate, in this moment, for this American people, can deliver ...

item.title: West Virginia sues Apple, accuses tech giant of letting iCloud become hub for child sexual abuse material
item.description: West Virginia becomes first state to sue Apple over allegations the tech giant allows predators to hide child abuse material in iCloud storage systems...

item.title: Pro-life group warns Republicans: Ditch abortion pill fight and pay at the polls
item.description: A leading pro-life group is warning Republicans that they could pay a political price in the midterms if the administration doesn't get tougher on abo...

item.title: Gov Wes Moore stands by account of grandfather's interaction with KKK amid scrutiny: 'Hurtful and offensive'
item.description: Maryland Gov. Wes Moore defends family KKK story after Washington Free Beacon report questions details about grandfather fleeing South Carolina threat...

item.title: Federal judge strikes down large parts of Trump mass detention policies for migrants
item.description: A federal judge blocked Trump deportation policies, ruling government's "worst of the worst" claim about detained migrants is inaccurate and overly br...

item.title: Just 5% of workers have this skill, according to Google—and they’re 4.5x times more likely to have a higher salary
item.description: The C-suite is pushing for AI productivity gains, but less than half of all workers are using the technology—or getting the proper training, according...

item.title: Mamdani’s Utopian Vision Faces Reality
item.description: The socialist superstar is already threatening to hike the property taxes of middle-class New Yorkers.

item.title: Pharma Ad Scrutiny a Test for Free Speech
item.description: Don’t let Washington decide what Americans are allowed to hear.

item.title: What The ‘Asian Grind Culture’ Panic Gets Wrong About American Education
item.description: Not all Asians are narrow-minded grinders, nor are all non-Asians free-spirited risk-takers.

item.title: Walmart exec says it’s ‘unfortunate’ that other companies are slashing workforces in the name of AI—they’re offering training to 1.6 million workers instead
item.description: Trillion-dollar retail giant Walmart says it’s not using AI to slash headcounts—instead, it’s providing workers with free access to Google AI training...

item.title: Rick Scott says Prince Andrew 'absolutely' must face US trial in Epstein case if American laws were broken
item.description: Sen. Rick Scott, R-Fla., said former Prince Andrew should "absolutely" stand trial in the U.S. if he is found to have broken American laws following h...

item.title: Royal Caribbean’s CEO is using AI to predict how many hamburgers you’ll eat—and to compete with Orlando, Vegas and Taylor Swift
item.description: "We're massive users of AI in a non-creepy way," Royal Caribbean CEO Jason Liberty told Fortune.

item.title: America’s Earliest Outdoor Photographs Shown at Hartford’s Wadsworth Atheneum
item.description: Your critic wanted to see this lovely show so much that he put his contempt for the museum’s trustees in abeyance for a day.

item.title: Trump's directive to increase glyphosate production faces MAHA backlash
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order on Wednesday to spur the domestic production of phosphorus and glyphosate-based herbicides by invoking the D...

item.title: ‘Greatest Country In The World’: USA Hockey Player Promotes Patriotism After OT Win
item.description: Some American athletes may be predisposed to trash their country on the world stage while competing in the 2026 Winter Olympics. But the same can’t be...

item.title: Amazon’s humble history: Jeff Bezos used a rented garage as an office and held team meetings at a local Barnes & Noble
item.description: Amazon's trajectory from a rented garage to a $2.4 trillion enterprise is now business legend.

item.title: Top Maryland Democrat defies Jeffries on mid-cycle redistricting push to boot state's lone Republican
item.description: A leading Maryland Democrat is resisting House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries’ push to redraw the state’s congressional map, exposing a rift over mid...

item.title: What the Market Knows That WACC Doesn’t
item.description: Market-implied discount rates reveal how investors price risk, often diverging from WACC and reshaping capital decisions. The post What the Market Kno...

item.title: Attention Bias in AI-Driven Investing
item.description: AI tools may favor popular stocks over overlooked ones, embedding attention bias into investment decisions. The post Attention Bias in AI-Driven Inves...

item.title: Social Security Claiming Strategies for High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Social Security claiming is a capital-allocation decision for affluent clients. This analysis weighs taxes, longevity risk, and liquidity trade-offs. ...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...
